title: 10 best herbal creams for jaw pain

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemongrass creams helps with jaw pain because of its unique combination of anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The natural oils present in lemongrass, such as citral and geraniol, have been shown to reduce inflammation and relax muscles, providing relief from tension headaches and jaw pain caused by teeth grinding or temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorders. Additionally, the aroma of lemongrass has a calming effect on the mind and body, further reducing stress and promoting overall well-being.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Lemongrass oil has anti-inflammatory compounds like citral, which helps reduce swelling and ease pain in the jaw, relieving symptoms of temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorder.

Analgesic properties: The analgesic compounds present in lemongrass, such as myrcene and geraniol, help numb the pain receptors in the jaw, providing instant relief from headaches, migraines, and TMJ-related pain.
Antispasmodic properties: Lemongrass oil's antispasmodic effects can help relax the muscles of the jaw, face, and neck, reducing tension and spasms that contribute to jaw pain and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, lemongrass roots help reduce inflammation and relieve tension in the jaw.
Rhyzomes: With its high concentration of citral and other essential oils, lemongrass rhyzomes provide a natural analgesic and antispasmodic effect to ease jaw pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh lemongrass leaves and stems measuring about one inch in length from the plant's base.
Dry the harvested lemongrass using a food dehydrator or air drying method on low heat for six hours.
Combine one tablespoon of dried lemongrass with one cup of coconut oil in a small saucepan over medium heat stirring constantly.
Infuse the mixture by allowing it to simmer for 20 minutes before straining and discarding the solids using cheesecloth or a fine mesh sieve.
Cool the infused oil to room temperature then whip it with one tablespoon of beeswax until a smooth creamy consistency is achieved.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Frankincense creams helps with jaw pain because of its potent anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The boswellic acids present in frankincense work by reducing inflammation and swelling in the joints, thereby alleviating tension and discomfort in the jaw muscles and ligaments. Additionally, frankincense's natural pain-relieving compounds help to soothe and relax the TMJ, allowing for a reduction in pain and stiffness associated with conditions like temporomandibular joint disorder (TMD). By targeting both inflammation and pain, frankincense creams provide effective relief from jaw pain without resorting to harsh chemicals.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Frankincense has anti-inflammatory compounds like boswellic acids, which reduce inflammation and swelling in the jaw area, providing relief from pain caused by conditions such as temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorder.

Antioxidant property: Frankincense's antioxidant properties help to neutralize free radicals that can cause oxidative stress and damage to tissues, including those in the jaw and face, thereby reducing inflammation and promoting healing.

Analgesic property: Frankincense has been shown to have analgesic (pain-relieving) effects by binding to opioid receptors in the brain, which helps to reduce pain perception and provide relief from jaw pain caused by conditions such as arthritis or dental problems.
remedy_parts: Roots: Containing anti-inflammatory compounds, frankincense roots help reduce swelling and relieve pain in the jaw area.
Rhyzomes: Rich in sesquiterpenes, which have analgesic properties, frankincense rhyzomes are used to ease toothache and gum pain.
Barks: With its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, frankincense bark is added to creams to reduce inflammation and promote healing in the jaw region.
remedy_recipe: Gather ingredients including 10g of dried boswellia serrata powder 5% glycerin 2% beeswax and 0.5% tea tree oil.
Combine 50ml of distilled water with 10g of dried boswellia serrata powder in a double boiler and steep for 30 minutes.
Remove from heat add 1g of 5% glycerin and 0.25g of beeswax and stir until the mixture reaches 60-70°c.
Pour the cooled mixture into sterilized glass jars and store in a cool dark place for at least 24 hours to allow gelation.
Apply a thin layer of the cream to the affected area as needed up to three times daily or as recommended by a healthcare professional.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wormwood creams helps with jaw pain because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which effectively reduce swelling and discomfort in the temporomandibular joint (TMJ). The cream's active ingredients, such as artemisinin and thujone, also have analgesic and antispasmodic effects, providing fast relief from aching and stiffness. By targeting the root causes of jaw pain, herbal wormwood creams can alleviate symptoms associated with temporomandibular disorders (TMD), allowing for improved oral function and reduced discomfort.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Wormwood cream's anti-inflammatory property helps reduce swelling and pain associated with temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorders, which often cause jaw pain.
Antispasmodic: Wormwood's antispasmodic properties help relax the muscles in the face, neck, and jaw, reducing spasms that can contribute to jaw pain and discomfort.
Analgesic: The analgesic compounds in wormwood cream help numb the affected area, providing relief from sharp, stabbing pains associated with TMJ disorders or dental problems.
remedy_parts: Roots: Wormwood roots contain a high concentration of bitter compounds, which help reduce inflammation and relax facial muscles associated with jaw pain.
Barks: The barks of the wormwood plant are rich in tannins, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm jaw discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality dried artemisia absinthium leaves measuring 2 tablespoons.
Combine the leaves with sweet almond oil in a double boiler at 100 degrees fahrenheit for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth into a clean glass container and discard solids.
Add beeswax pellets and vitamin e oil to the liquid extract, heating until melted and smooth.
Pour the cream into sterilized jars, seal tightly, and store in the refrigerator for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tea tree creams helps with jaw pain because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties. The natural oil, terpinen-4-ol, in tea tree oil reduces swelling and relieves tension in the jaw muscles, which often contribute to migraines, TMJ disorders, and bruxism-related pain. Additionally, tea tree cream's soothing effects calm the skin around the jawline, allowing for a more relaxed facial expression, which can also help alleviate jaw strain. By targeting both internal and external sources of discomfort, tea tree creams offer a holistic approach to managing jaw pain.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Tea tree cream's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as terpinen-4-ol, help reduce swelling and alleviate pain caused by temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorders.

Antimicrobial properties: The antimicrobial properties of tea tree oil in the cream can help prevent infection and promote healing of inflamed or irritated tissue in the jaw area, reducing pain and discomfort.

Analgesic and anesthetic properties: Compounds like menthol and camphor in herbal tea tree creams can numb the affected area, providing temporary relief from jaw pain and making it easier to manage symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the tea tree plant contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce pain and inflammation in the jaw area.
Barks: Tea tree bark is rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which have analgesic properties that can provide relief from jaw pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh melaleuca alternifolia leaves in early morning or evening to ensure optimal potency.
Dry the harvested leaves using air circulation method for 7-10 days to reduce moisture content.
Combine 20% of dried melaleuca alternifolia leaves with 80% coconut oil and vitamin e oil in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture under low heat for 6 hours, then filter it through cheesecloth to obtain a liquid extract.
Mix the extract with beeswax and shea butter, heat until melted, then pour into molds to set and cool.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tasmanian blue gum creams helps with jaw pain because they contain a unique combination of herbs that work together to reduce inflammation and relax tense muscles. The cream's anti-inflammatory properties help to soothe sore joints and reduce stiffness, while its analgesic effects provide fast-acting relief from pain. Additionally, the cream's ability to calm irritated nerves helps to break the cycle of chronic tension and pain in the jaw, providing long-lasting relief for those suffering from temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorder or other jaw-related conditions.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Tasmanian Blue Gum creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce swelling and inflammation in the jaw muscles, relieving tension and pain caused by conditions like temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorder.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in Tasmanian Blue Gum creams help protect the jaw muscles from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to chronic jaw pain and stiffness.

Pain-relieving terpenes: The terpene compounds found in Tasmanian Blue Gum creams, such as eucalyptol and globulol, have been shown to have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to numb the pain and reduce discomfort associated with jaw pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, Tasmanian blue gum roots have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making them an effective ingredient in medicinal creams for jaw pain relief.
Barks: The bark of the Tasmanian blue gum plant contains salicin, a compound similar to aspirin, which helps to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain associated with jaw tension.
remedy_recipe: Sterilize equipment and workspace by autoclaving at 121°c for 15 minutes to ensure cleanliness.
Combine 250g of high-quality beeswax and 100g of coconut oil in a heat-proof mixing bowl.
Melt the mixture over low heat, stirring occasionally, for approximately 30-40 minutes until fully incorporated.
Add 20ml of eucalyptus essential oil, 10% concentration, to the melted mixture and stir well to combine.
Pour the mixture into sterilized glass containers, allowing it to cool and set at room temperature for at least 24 hours before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage creams helps with jaw pain because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm the affected area. The cream's active ingredients, including salvia officinalis, work to reduce swelling and ease tension in the jaw muscles, providing relief from discomfort and pain. Additionally, sage's antioxidants help to neutralize free radicals that can exacerbate inflammation, promoting a faster recovery and reducing the likelihood of chronic issues. By using sage creams, individuals with jaw pain can experience a reduction in discomfort and improved overall oral health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Sage creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds like rosmarinic acid, which can help reduce swelling and discomfort in the jaw joint and surrounding tissues, alleviating tension and pain.
Muscle relaxant: Sage has been shown to possess muscle-relaxing properties, which can help ease spasms and tense muscles in the jaw, neck, and face that contribute to jaw pain and discomfort.

Antioxidant: The antioxidant compounds present in sage creams, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, can help protect against oxidative stress and cell damage, which may contribute to chronic pain and inflammation in the jaw.
remedy_parts: Roots: Sage roots contain a high concentration of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in reducing swelling and discomfort associated with jaw pain.
Rhyzomes: The rhizome of the sage plant is rich in essential oils, which help to relax tense muscles and ease tension headaches that can contribute to jaw pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried leaves of salvia officinalis and dry them completely according to your desired method for at least 24 hours.
Combine 20 grams of dried salvia officinalis leaves with 100 milliliters of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil in a clean glass jar.
Infuse the mixture in a warm dark place for 14 days, shaking the jar daily to ensure even extraction, allowing for optimal flavonoid and saponin release.
Strain the infused mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding the solids and reserving the liquid extract.

Combine 20 milliliters of the salvia officinalis extract with 100 grams of beeswax and 50 milliliters of carrier oil in a double boiler, heating until melted and blended for a smooth cream consistency within 30 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender creams helps with jaw pain because of its soothing and calming properties. The natural oils in lavender, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, have been shown to reduce muscle spasms and tension, which are common causes of jaw pain. When applied topically, the cream can ease inflammation and discomfort in the jaw joint, allowing for a reduction in pain and stiffness. Additionally, the calming effects of lavender can promote relaxation, further reducing stress and anxiety that may contribute to jaw tension.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Lavender's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, help reduce swelling and pain in the jaw muscles and joints, alleviating tension and discomfort.

Analgesic properties: The analgesic components of lavender, including linalool and camphor, work to numb the affected area, providing relief from acute or chronic jaw pain caused by conditions like temporomandibular joint disorder (TMJ).

Muscle relaxant properties: Lavender's sedative and relaxing compounds, such as linalyl acetate and beta-caryophyllene, help calm tense muscles in the face and jaw, reducing spasms and alleviating pain associated with conditions like bruxism or teeth grinding.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in calcium and magnesium, the roots of the English lavender plant help to reduce inflammation and relax muscle tension, making them effective in alleviating jaw pain.

Seeds: Containing a high concentration of fatty acids, the seeds of the English lavender plant provide anti-inflammatory properties that can help to soothe and calm the muscles responsible for jaw pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh lavender flowers from lavandula angustifolia plants in the morning after dew has evaporated, gathering at least 2 ounces of dried petals.
Combine the dried lavender petals with 8 fluid ounces of sweet almond oil and 4 fluid ounces of beeswax in a double boiler, heating for 20-25 minutes.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter to remove solids, then add 1 teaspoon of vitamin e oil and 1/2 teaspoon of calendula extract.
Whisk in 2 tablespoons of shea butter and 1 tablespoon of coconut oil until smooth and creamy, taking approximately 10-15 minutes.
Pour the cream mixture into sterilized glass jars, allowing to cool at room temperature for at least 30 minutes before refrigerating or freezing.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Damask rose creams helps with jaw pain because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce swelling and discomfort in the joints and muscles associated with temporomandibular disorder (TMD). The cream's soothing and calming effects also help to relax tense muscles and improve circulation, reducing tension in the face and jaw. Additionally, damask rose has a natural ability to reduce stress and anxiety, which are common triggers of TMD. By applying the cream topically, individuals can experience relief from jaw pain and discomfort.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Damask rose creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds like salicylic acid, which help reduce inflammation and swelling in the temporomandibular joint (TMJ), alleviating jaw pain caused by conditions like TMJ disorder.

Antioxidants: The antioxidants present in damask rose creams, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, help protect the tissues of the TMJ from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to chronic jaw pain and stiffness.

Soothing and calming properties: Damask rose creams have a soothing and calming effect on the skin and mucous membranes, making them effective in reducing discomfort, tension, and pain associated with jaw clenching or teeth grinding disorders.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain a high concentration of essential oils and bioactive compounds that help reduce inflammation and ease muscle tension in the jaw.
Flowers: The flowers are rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help soothe jaw pain and reduce swelling.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry rosa damascena flowers, using approximately 20 grams of dried petals per 100ml of cream.
Infuse the dried petals in hot water for 10 minutes, resulting in a fragrant and potent floral decoction.
Combine the infused liquid with 20% beeswax and 80% sweet almond oil, melting the mixture at 60°c for 15 minutes.
Strain the cooled mixture through cheesecloth to remove any solids, yielding a smooth and consistent cream base.
Whip the cream base into a light and fluffy consistency using an electric mixer on low speed for 2-3 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme creams helps with jaw pain because it contains natural anti-inflammatory compounds that soothe and calm the muscles in the face and neck. The aromatic properties of thyme also help to relax tense jaw muscles, reducing tension and discomfort. Additionally, thyme's antimicrobial properties can help to combat infections and inflammation that may be contributing to chronic jaw pain. By applying a thyme cream to the affected area, individuals can experience quick and effective relief from jaw pain and tension headaches.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic: Thyme's antispasmodic properties help to relax muscles and reduce spasms, which can contribute to jaw tension and pain.
Anti-inflammatory: Thyme's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as thymol, can help reduce swelling and pain in the jaw joint and surrounding tissues.
Antimicrobial: Thyme's antimicrobial properties can help combat bacterial infections that may be contributing to jaw pain, such as abscesses or infected gums.
remedy_parts: Roots: Thyme roots have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and pain in the jaw area, making them effective for treating temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorders.
Barks: Thyme barks contain flavonoids and tannins that work as natural analgesics, providing relief from jaw pain and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh thymus vulgaris leaves and flowers, ensuring adequate supply for the required amount of cream preparation.
Dry the thymus vulgaris plant material in a warm air circulation oven at 35°c for two hours or until crumbly texture achieved.
Combine dried thymus vulgaris with beeswax, coconut oil, and jojoba oil in a double boiler setup, heating mixture to 65°c while stirring constantly.
Add 5% of vitamin e oil and 2% of tea tree oil to the mixture, allowing it to cool down to 50°c before whipping until creamy consistency achieved.
Fill sterilized cream jars with the prepared thymus vulgaris cream, sealing tightly and labeling for storage in a dark place at room temperature for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold creams helps with jaw pain because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The cream's active ingredients, such as calendula extract, work to reduce swelling and ease tension in the affected area. This can provide relief from symptoms associated with temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorder, such as clicking or locking jaws, facial pain, and limited jaw movement. By soothing and calming the muscles and joints, pot marigold creams can help restore normal function and reduce discomfort, promoting a healthier and more comfortable jaw.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Pot marigold contains a compound called saponin, which has anti-inflammatory effects that can help reduce swelling and pain in the jaw.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in pot marigold cream, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, can help protect against tissue damage caused by free radicals and promote healing of inflamed tissues.

Anti-spasmodic properties: Pot marigold has been traditionally used to relax muscles and relieve spasms, which can be beneficial in reducing jaw pain caused by conditions such as temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorder.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and ease pain in the jaw.
Buds: The buds contain antioxidants that help reduce inflammation and promote healing, making them effective in soothing jaw pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried calendula flowers measuring 20 grams and grind them into fine powder.
Combine the powdered calendula with 100 milliliters of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil or coconut oil at a ratio of 1:5.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler for 30 minutes to facilitate infusion and penetration of active compounds.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth into a clean container discarding the solids and let it cool to room temperature.
Whip the cooled infusion with 10 grams of beeswax and 20 milliliters of aloe vera gel until smooth and creamy.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint creams helps with jaw pain because of its natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties. The menthol present in peppermint relaxes the jaw muscles, reducing tension and stiffness. Additionally, the cooling sensation from the cream can help to numb the affected area, providing temporary relief from discomfort. As a result, herbal peppermint creams can provide quick and effective relief for individuals experiencing jaw pain due to conditions such as temporomandibular joint disorder (TMJ) or bruxism.
remedy_properties: Eugenol: Eugenol has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which can help to reduce swelling and ease pain in the jaw muscles and joints.
Menthol: Menthol helps to cool and numb the affected area, providing fast relief from pain and discomfort caused by temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorders or bruxism (teeth grinding).

Volatile oils: The volatile oils present in peppermint creams, such as menthone and limonene, can help to relax muscle tension and reduce spasms in the jaw muscles, which is common in conditions like TMJ disorder.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in menthol and other bioactive compounds, peppermint roots help to reduce inflammation and ease muscle tension associated with jaw pain.
Barks: The bark of the peppermint plant contains flavonoids and phenolic acids that have anti-inflammatory properties, making it effective in soothing sore jaw muscles.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh mint leaves in large quantities for processing.
Dry the harvested mint leaves using air circulation method.
Grind dried mint leaves into fine powder using mortar and pestle.
Melt coconut oil in a double boiler to create base cream.
Combine ground mint powder with melted coconut oil and mix well.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's wort creams helps with jaw pain because they contain a natural ingredient that has analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties. When applied topically to the affected area, it can help reduce muscle tension and discomfort in the temporomandibular joint (TMJ), which is often the source of jaw pain. Additionally, St John's wort creams may also help to relax facial muscles, reducing the frequency and severity of jaw pain episodes. This natural remedy provides relief from chronic jaw pain without resorting to medication or invasive treatments.
remedy_properties: Antidepressant properties: St. John's Wort cream contains hyperforin and adhyperforin, which are believed to have antidepressant effects by increasing serotonin and noradrenaline levels in the brain, helping to reduce tension and alleviate jaw pain.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The flavonoids and polyphenols present in St. John's Wort cream have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help to reduce swelling and inflammation associated with temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorders, a common cause of jaw pain.

Pain-relieving properties: St. John's Wort cream contains hypericin, which has been shown to have analgesic and sedative effects, helping to numb the pain and reduce discomfort in the jaw and surrounding areas.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used to create a calming and anti-inflammatory effect, which helps to reduce jaw pain and discomfort.
Barks: The barks contain tannins that help to reduce swelling and inflammation in the jaw area, providing relief from pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh hypericum perforatum leaves and flowers and dry them completely using shade or air drying for several weeks.
Grind dried hypericum perforatum into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for about 30 minutes.
Mix the powdered hypericum perforatum with coconut oil in a 1:5 ratio, stirring well to create a smooth paste.
Combine the hypericum perforatum-coconut oil mixture with beeswax and vitamin e oil in a double boiler, heating gently until melted and well combined.
Pour the warm cream into sterilized glass jars, allowing it to cool and solidify before storing in a cool dry place for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian creams helps with jaw pain because they contain the essential oil of valeriana officinalis, which has natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties. When applied topically to the affected area, the cream can help reduce muscle tension and ease spasms in the jaw muscles, providing relief from conditions such as temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorder, bruxism, and migraines. The cream's ability to promote relaxation and reduce stress also contributes to its effectiveness in alleviating jaw pain, making it a popular natural remedy for many people.
remedy_properties: Anxiolytic and Sedative Properties: Valerian root has a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help to reduce anxiety and stress-related jaw tension, thus alleviating jaw pain.

Anti-Inflammatory Properties: Valerian root contains compounds that have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help to reduce swelling and pain in the jaw muscles and joints caused by conditions like temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorder.

Muscle Relaxant Properties: Valerian root has a natural muscle-relaxing effect, which can help to soothe tense jaw muscles and relax the jaw, reducing pain and discomfort associated with clenching or grinding of the teeth.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in valerenic acid, which has anti-inflammatory and sedative properties, making it effective in reducing jaw pain and discomfort.
Rhizomes: Contain sesquiterpenes, which have analgesic and antispasmodic effects, helping to alleviate tension headaches and jaw pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry valeriana officinalis roots, weighing approximately 200 grams, for three to four weeks.
Grind the dried valeriana officinalis roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle, producing a quantity of approximately 100 grams.
Combine the ground valeriana officinalis root powder with 400 milliliters of coconut oil and 200 milliliters of beeswax in a heat-resistant mixing bowl.
Heat the mixture over low temperature for two hours, stirring occasionally, to facilitate the infusion of the herbal extract into the carrier oils.
Pour the warm cream into sterilized glass jars, allowing it to cool and solidify at room temperature before storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Bitter orange creams helps with jaw pain because of their potent anti-inflammatory properties, which effectively reduce swelling and discomfort in the temporomandibular joint (TMJ). The cream's active ingredients, such as limonene and hesperidin, work to relax tense muscles and improve blood flow to the affected area. This alleviates tension and pressure on the jaw joints, providing quick relief from pain and stiffness associated with conditions like TMJ disorder, bruxism, or teeth grinding.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: Herbal bitter orange creams contain antioxidants like flavonoids, which may help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the muscles and joints, including those involved in temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorders.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The citrus peel extracts in bitter orange creams may exhibit anti-inflammatory effects by reducing prostaglandins, a type of hormone that contributes to pain and swelling in conditions like TMJ disorders.

Muscle relaxant activity: Herbal bitter orange creams may contain compounds that relax muscle spasms and reduce tension, which can help alleviate jaw pain and stiffness associated with conditions like temporomandibular disorders (TMD).
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in flavonoids and limonoids, which provide anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that help relieve jaw pain.
Barks: Containing phenolic compounds and terpenes, which exhibit antioxidant and antimicrobial activities, making them effective in soothing jaw discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30g of dried citrus x aurantium peel and grind into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle within 2 hours.
Mix 10g of grated beeswax, 15g of coconut oil, and 5g of sweet almond oil in a heat-resistant bowl within 1 minute.
Add 20 drops of citrus x aurantium essential oil and the ground peel powder to the oil mixture and stir until well combined within 2 minutes.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler for 10 minutes or until the beeswax has melted, then remove from heat and let cool to room temperature within 15 minutes.
Stir the cooled mixture every 5 minutes as it thickens over the next hour until it reaches a creamy consistency, then transfer to an airtight container for storage within 60 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice creams helps with jaw pain because they contain a compound called glycyrrhizin, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can effectively soothe and relax the muscles of the face and jaw. This natural ingredient can help reduce tension and stiffness in the temporomandibular joint (TMJ), alleviating symptoms such as jaw clicking, grinding, and locking. Additionally, licorice creams can also calm inflammation and irritation caused by conditions like bruxism, TMJ disorder, or arthritis, providing quick and lasting relief from jaw pain and discomfort.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: This compound helps reduce inflammation and relaxes muscles, which can alleviate tension-related jaw pain and discomfort.
Flavonoids: These antioxidants have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which can help soothe and calm the nerves and soft tissues in the jaw, reducing pain and stiffness.

Liquiritin: This compound has been shown to reduce inflammation and relax muscles, similar to glycyrrhizin, but also has a specific affinity for the trigeminal nerve, which is responsible for transmitting pain signals from the jaw area.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the licorice plant contain glycyrrhizin, which has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and pain in the jaw.
Barks: The barks of the licorice plant have been traditionally used to treat toothaches and gum problems, making them a valuable ingredient in medicinal creams for jaw pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 cups of dried licorice root powder and grind it into a fine texture using a mortar and pestle for 15 minutes.
Mix the ground licorice root with 1 cup of beeswax pellets, 1/2 cup of coconut oil, and 1 tablespoon of vitamin e oil in a heat-proof bowl for 5 minutes.
Place the mixture in a double boiler and heat it to 120°f over low heat for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally until smooth and creamy.
Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool to 100°f before adding 2 tablespoons of glycerin and 1 teaspoon of peppermint essential oil; stir well for 5 minutes.

Pour the mixture into sterilized jars and label them with the date, ingredients, and instructions for use, allowing the cream to solidify at room temperature for 30 minutes before storage for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chinese dogwood creams helps with jaw pain because they contain natural anti-inflammatory compounds that penetrate deep into the muscles and joints, providing fast and effective relief from tension and discomfort. The cream's soothing properties also help to calm irritated tissues, reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation in the facial muscles and joints. As a result, individuals suffering from temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorders or jaw pain can enjoy improved mobility, reduced stiffness, and a significant decrease in pain levels with regular use of herbal Chinese dogwood creams.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Herbal Chinese Dogwood creams contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce swelling and ease pain in the jaw and surrounding areas.

Pain-relieving properties: The creams often include analgesic herbs like ginger and turmeric, which contain compounds that inhibit the production of pain mediators, providing relief from jaw pain and discomfort.

Muscle relaxant properties: Certain ingredients in the creams, such as peppermint and wintergreen, have a calming effect on muscles and can help reduce tension and spasms in the jaw, neck, and face, which are common contributors to jaw pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Chinese dogwood are rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, which help to reduce swelling and ease discomfort in the jaw.
Barks: The barks of Chinese dogwood contain flavonoids that have analgesic properties, providing relief from jaw pain and inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest cornus officinalis roots and dry them thoroughly in a warm oven at 150°f for 2 hours.
Grind 10 grams of dried cornus officinalis root into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered root with 50 grams of coconut oil and 20 grams of beeswax in a heat-proof glass bowl.
Heat the mixture over low heat, stirring constantly, for 30 minutes or until the beeswax is fully dissolved.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into sterilized glass jars and let it cool before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sweet lavender creams helps with jaw pain because they possess a unique combination of soothing properties that work together to reduce discomfort. The calming essence of lavender oil relaxes tense facial muscles, while the sweet fragrance helps to calm the nervous system, reducing anxiety and stress-related jaw clenching. Additionally, the cream's emollient texture provides gentle, long-lasting relief to the affected area, helping to alleviate pain and inflammation associated with temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorders.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Lavender's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce swelling and pain in the jaw joint, making it more effective at alleviating symptoms of temporomandibular disorder (TMD).

Anxiolytic: Sweet lavender has been shown to have anxiolytic effects, which can help calm tension and stress that may be contributing to jaw clenching and teeth grinding, both common causes of jaw pain.

Muscle relaxant: Lavender's muscle-relaxing properties can help soothe and calm the muscles in the face and neck, which are often involved in jaw pain and TMD, providing relief from tension and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in reducing swelling and pain associated with jaw inflammation.
Flowers: Containing calming properties, lavender flowers can help ease tension and anxiety-related jaw clenching.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh lavandula latifolia leaves at dawn and dry them using shade drying or air circulation for 2 weeks.
Combine 20g of dried lavandula latifolia leaves with 40ml of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil in a small saucepan.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes while gently stirring to infuse the essential oils into the carrier oil.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass container and discard the solids after 1 hour.
Mix 10ml of the infused lavandula latifolia oil with 20ml of beeswax and 50ml of coconut oil in a double boiler for 10 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lime creams helps with jaw pain because it contains natural anti-inflammatory compounds that effectively soothe and calm the jaw muscles. The combination of herbal extracts, such as willow bark and ginger, works to reduce inflammation and ease tension in the temporomandibular joint (TMJ), which is a common cause of jaw pain. Additionally, the creamy texture of lime creams provides a gentle, cooling sensation that can help relax the jaw muscles and provide instant relief from discomfort.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds (e.g. limonoids, flavonoids): These compounds help to reduce swelling and alleviate inflammation in the jaw joints and muscles, which is often a contributing factor to chronic pain.

Anxiolytic and sedative properties: The herbal extracts in lime creams, such as passionflower or lemon balm, can help to calm anxiety and promote relaxation, reducing tension in the jaw muscles that can exacerbate jaw pain.
Pain-relieving terpenes (e.g. limonene, citral): These natural compounds have analgesic and anti-nociceptive properties, helping to reduce pain and discomfort in the jaw area.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the lime plant contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making them effective in relieving jaw pain.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of the lime plant have been traditionally used to treat a variety of ailments, including dental issues like toothaches and gum inflammation, which can contribute to jaw pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh tilia x europaea flowers and leaves in the morning for maximum potency.
Dry the harvested plant material using shade drying or low-temperature oven at 35°c for 24 hours.
Combine 30 grams of dried tilia x europaea flowers and 20 grams of dried tilia x europaea leaves with 100 ml of olive oil in a glass jar.
Steep the mixture at room temperature for 14 days, shaking the jar daily to facilitate infusion.
Strain the liquid using cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container, discarding the solids and transferring the infused oil to dark glass jars for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe creams helps with jaw pain because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The gel inside aloe leaves contains a powerful compound called aloin, which reduces swelling and relaxes muscles in the face and jaw. When applied topically to the affected area, aloe cream can help alleviate tension headaches and migraines caused by temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorder. Its cooling effect also helps to reduce pain and discomfort associated with bruxism, grinding or clenching of teeth, and other jaw-related conditions.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Aloe vera's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling and pain caused by temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorders, allowing for easier movement and relief from discomfort.
Antioxidant: The antioxidant activity in herbal aloe creams helps protect the TMJ and surrounding tissues from oxidative stress and damage, which can exacerbate jaw pain and stiffness.

Analgesic: The analgesic compounds present in aloe vera cream help numb the affected area, providing quick relief from acute or chronic jaw pain, including toothaches and headaches caused by TMJ disorders.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Containing anti-inflammatory compounds like aloin and aloe-emodin, leaves help soothe and reduce swelling associated with jaw pain.
Rhyzomes: Rich in vitamins, minerals, and antioxidants, rhyzomes contribute to the cream's ability to promote healing and reduce inflammation in the affected area.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh aloe vera leaves and cut them into small pieces to release the gel.
Combine one cup of the extracted aloe vera gel with two tablespoons of coconut oil in a saucepan.
Add four drops of peppermint essential oil and three drops of eucalyptus essential oil to the mixture.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 10-15 minutes, stirring occasionally, until it reaches a smooth consistency.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a glass jar, allowing it to cool before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Nutmeg creams helps with jaw pain because of its anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The cream's active ingredients, such as cinnamaldehyde and eugenol, penetrate deep into the tissues to reduce swelling and ease discomfort in the jaw muscles. Additionally, nutmeg's natural warming sensation can help relax tense jaw muscles, promoting a sense of calm and relaxation. By targeting both inflammation and muscle tension, herbal nutmeg creams provide effective relief from chronic jaw pain, making them a popular treatment option for conditions like temporomandibular joint disorder (TMJ).
remedy_properties: Cannabidiol (CBD): CBD has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which may help reduce muscle tension and relieve jaw pain caused by temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorders.

Peppermint oil: Peppermint oil has a cooling effect when applied topically, which can help ease tension in the jaw muscles and provide temporary relief from pain associated with bruxism or TMJ disorders.

Arnica: Arnica has anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce swelling and alleviate pain in the jaw and surrounding areas, making it potentially beneficial for treating jaw pain caused by injuries or dental procedures.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in natural compounds that help reduce inflammation and alleviate pain, making them a valuable component in medicinal creams for jaw pain.
Rhyzomes: Containing essential oils with analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, rhyzomes can effectively soothe and calm the affected area.
Buds: Small but potent, nutmeg buds are rich in antioxidants and have been shown to reduce swelling and ease discomfort associated with jaw pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried myristica fragrans seeds from a reputable supplier.
Grind the seeds into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Combine 20g of the powdered seeds with 50ml of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, to infuse the oil with the herbal extract.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth and mix with 10% beeswax to create a smooth cream.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for jaw pain are topical preparations containing natural ingredients that soothe and relax muscles, reducing tension and discomfort associated with temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorders. These creams typically feature a blend of herbs such as arnica, lavender, and peppermint, which work together to ease muscle spasms, reduce inflammation, and promote relaxation. Examples include products like Biotène Cream and Orajel Cream, which have been shown to provide significant relief from jaw pain, allowing individuals to eat, speak, and sleep more comfortably. This can greatly improve daily life by reducing stress, anxiety, and frustration associated with chronic jaw pain.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat jaw pain with herbal creams is by using a blend of anti-inflammatory and analgesic herbs such as willow bark, ginger, and arnica. Apply the cream topically to the affected area, massaging it gently into the skin until fully absorbed. Repeat as needed throughout the day for optimal relief from tension and discomfort associated with temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorders or migraines.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to jaw pain that are treated with herbal creams are conditions such as headaches, migraines, and facial tension. Herbal creams containing ingredients like arnica, peppermint, and chamomile can help soothe and relax these areas, reducing discomfort and inflammation. Additionally, herbal creams may also be used to treat muscle strains, sprains, and arthritis-related pain in the neck, shoulder, and back regions.
